subject: Interview scheduling — Anya Chen, Sr. Frontend Engineer

messages.subject: Interview scheduling — Sr. Frontend Engineer role at Vectrix
messages.body:
Hi Anya,

Thank you for completing the take-home assignment. We were genuinely impressed — the P95/P99 latency chart implementation was exactly the kind of thinking we're looking for.

We'd like to move forward with the final panel, which consists of:
- Technical deep dive (60 min): Marcus Webb + Liam Nakamura
- Product/culture panel (60 min): Dana Reeves + Priya Chandran

We can combine into a half-day on the same day, or run them back to back with a short break. Would February 27 work? We're flexible on the time slot.

Nina Kowalski (CC'd) manages our scheduling — she'll coordinate once we have a date confirmed.

— Marcus Webb
CTO, Vectrix Labs

messages.subject: Re: Interview scheduling — Sr. Frontend Engineer role at Vectrix
messages.body:
Hi Marcus,

Thank you — I'm glad the take-home landed well. I had a lot of fun with the latency chart problem.

Feb 27 works well for me. I'd prefer the combined half-day if possible — I can do 10am–1pm PT with a 15-minute break between the two panels. Does that work for your team?

— Anya

messages.subject: Re: Interview scheduling — calendar invite sent
messages.body:
Hi Anya,

Perfect — 10am–1pm PT on Feb 27 is confirmed. Sending the calendar invite now.

Schedule:
- 10:00–11:00am: Technical deep dive (Marcus Webb + Liam Nakamura) — Zoom
- 11:00–11:15am: Break
- 11:15am–12:15pm: Product/culture panel (Dana Reeves + Priya Chandran) — Zoom

Zoom link is in the invite. Please let me know if you need anything — happy to answer any logistics questions.

— Nina
Ops & People, Vectrix Labs
